section: Everyday Line — Core Black product card
prompt_used: Premium studio product photography of the TrailVolt Everyday 22L backpack in Core Black. Shot at a confident 3/4 angle showing the front face and one side panel. Background: dark navy (#0B1626) seamless. Lighting: three-point setup, key light from upper-right at 45 degrees, soft fill from left, very thin cool rim light catching the shoulder strap edge and top handle. Shell: 600D ballistic nylon, visible crosshatch weave, matte surface with subtle depth. Module attachment rail: a horizontal row of 6 matte gunmetal connection slots across the mid front face — the defining product feature, sharply in focus. YKK zipper pulls: matte black, precise. Integrated branding: on the upper-left area of the front panel, just below the top handle attachment point, a small matte black embossed rubber badge approximately 40mm wide, bearing the TrailVolt inverted triangle symbol above the 'TrailVolt' wordmark — the badge is the same matte black as the surrounding fabric, nearly flush with the surface, visible only because the light catches its slight relief. This is how premium outdoor brands brand their products: silent, structural, premium. The badge is NOT a sticker, NOT a printed label, NOT a floating overlay — it is a physical rubber element moulded into the panel. No other text. No other branding. Studio quality, hyper-sharp across the front face, commercial product photography at its finest.

section: Everyday Line — Slate Grey product card
prompt_used: Premium studio product photography of the TrailVolt Everyday 22L backpack in Slate Grey (#6B7280). Shot at the exact same 3/4 angle as the Core Black version — collection consistency is critical. Background: dark navy (#0B1626) seamless. Same three-point lighting setup. Slate Grey colourway: a deliberate cool grey-blue, clearly lighter than the dark navy background, clearly distinct from Core Black — not silver, not charcoal, not military grey. A calm, professional tone. Shell: 600D ballistic nylon, the crosshatch weave visible in a slightly softer way than on black fabric due to the lighter base tone. Module attachment rail: same matte stainless horizontal connection slots across the front face — they contrast more cleanly against the grey fabric than on black, giving this version excellent rail visibility. YKK zipper pulls: matte gunmetal grey, chosen to match the colourway. Integrated branding: on the upper-left front panel, a small gunmetal grey embossed rubber badge approximately 40mm wide, bearing the TrailVolt inverted triangle and wordmark — the badge is slightly darker than the slate fabric, creating a subtle tonal contrast. The badge is a physical moulded rubber element, flush to the panel surface, not a print or sticker. It is small, premium, and reads as intentional product design. No text anywhere else on the pack. Studio quality, commercially sharp.

section: Travel Line — Core Black product card
prompt_used: Premium studio product photography of the TrailVolt Travel 30L backpack in Core Black. 3/4 angle showing the front face and left side panel — the side profile is important here because it reveals the clamshell zip. Background: dark navy (#0B1626) seamless. Three-point lighting, key from upper-right. The Travel 30L is visibly taller and wider than the Everyday 22L — this is the largest pack in the range and it reads as such. Front face features: the module attachment rail in the lower-mid area (matte gunmetal connection slots), and the upper rapid-access organiser zip. Side profile: the continuous clamshell zip wraps from the base, up the left side, and across the top — this is the pack's signature feature for flat-open access and carry-on packing. Shell material: 420D Ripstop Nylon with DWR treatment, the ripstop diamond grid faintly visible in the surface texture. Matte Core Black throughout. Lockable zip pulls: a slightly different profile from standard YKK pulls — these have a small security-lock loop. Integrated branding: on the upper-right area of the front panel, above the organiser zip, a small matte black embossed rubber badge approximately 40mm wide bearing the TrailVolt inverted triangle above the wordmark — same construction as the Everyday badge: a moulded rubber element, flush to the panel, tonal against the black fabric, visible from the light catching its relief. Not a sticker. Not a print. Not an overlay. Premium, structural branding. No text anywhere else.

section: Trail Line — Forest Green product card
prompt_used: Premium studio product photography of the TrailVolt Trail 28L backpack in Forest Green (#2D5016). 3/4 angle showing front face and one side panel — same collection angle as the Everyday and Travel cards. Background: dark navy (#0B1626) seamless. Three-point lighting, key from upper-right, soft fill. The Trail 28L is visibly different from the Everyday: slightly taller top rise, more pronounced load-bearing shoulder harness, reinforced top haul handle. Shell: 500D Cordura, slightly rougher surface texture than the 600D nylon of the Everyday — the weave is more open and the fabric reads as genuinely technical outdoor material. Forest Green (#2D5016): a deep natural green, not olive, not khaki, not military camo — the colour of mature hedgerow, Irish field margin, Highland moorland grass in overcast light. It is unmistakably green and unmistakably deliberate. Module attachment rail on the front face: same horizontal connection slots in matte gunmetal, contrasting cleanly against the green fabric. YKK zipper pulls: matte black. Integrated branding: on the upper-left front panel, a small woven fabric badge approximately 45mm wide — a rectangular woven label with a dark forest green ground and the TrailVolt inverted triangle and wordmark rendered in a slightly lighter sage or grey-green thread. This is a woven textile element, not a rubber moulding — it reads as outdoor heritage brand construction, like Patagonia or Arc'teryx woven patches. The badge is stitched flat to the panel, with visible stitching around its perimeter. No other text. No other branding. The word 'TRAILVOLT' does NOT appear printed or embossed across the pack face or body — only on the small woven label. Studio quality.

Square-format ultra-premium studio product photography of the TrailVolt Everyday 22L Core Pack in Core Black. The camera angle is near-frontal — the pack faces almost directly toward the viewer with just a 10-15 degree tilt to the left, creating depth while keeping the entire front face in sharp focus. This is the definitive product image used in the product configurator: a buyer actively deciding which modules to add needs to see every detail of the front face with absolute clarity. Background: dark navy (#0B1626). An extremely subtle electric mint glow originates from directly behind the pack, catching only the very bottom edge — a whisper of the 'technology is alive' signal. Module attachment rail: the central, dominant feature. A horizontal row of 6 rectangular matte gunmetal connection slots across the middle of the front face. Each slot approximately 15mm wide in real scale, with visible depth, machined edges, and a small locating tab at each side. The buyer is mentally placing modules here. The slot geometry must be ultra-sharp. Rapid-access front organiser zip: a horizontal zip across the upper front face, matte YKK hardware. Main compartment wrap-around zip running the perimeter. Shoulder strap tops emerging at the upper frame. Shell: 600D ballistic nylon, crosshatch weave pattern visible at 100% zoom — the buyer can zoom in and count threads. Integrated branding: just above and to the right of the module attachment rail, a small brushed anodized aluminium nameplate approximately 38mm wide, debossed with the TrailVolt inverted triangle mark above the wordmark. The nameplate is approximately 1mm proud of the fabric surface, with a satin finish — not chrome, not mirror. It reads as precision engineering: the same attention to detail as the module rail slots themselves. This is a machined component, not a sticker or print. No other text on the pack. No props. No hands. Focal length: 85mm equivalent. Ultra-sharp front face.
